preview_text:
The model favors Bosnia and Herzegovina with a 47% win probability, but the gap over Qatar is modest—only 24 percentage points separate the two. The underdog Qatar registers a 23% chance, suggesting this is far from a foregone conclusion on the statistical forecast.

The draw sits at 31% in the model, the most plausible single outcome alongside a Bosnia win. The relatively compressed outcome probabilities indicate a competitive match; no result is heavily favored, and the margin between favorite and underdog leaves room for either side to prevail given normal variance in open play.

The market consensus—both sportsbooks and Polymarket—diverges notably from the model. Market sources place Bosnia and Herzegovina at 58%, a 12 percentage point lift, while pushing Qatar down to 17%. Both retail and crowd-sourced pricing align closely with each other but notably tighten the Bosnia edge and compress draw probability to 25–26%. The dashboard does not claim to know which assessment is correct; the divergence flags a material disagreement about relative team strength.
